Changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) is managed by DPIIT.
It provides financial assistance to early-stage startups.
Aims to foster entrepreneurship in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of government-backed seed funding schemes like SISFS in nurturing India's startup ecosystem and promoting innovation, particularly in non-metro cities.
Discuss the challenges faced by early-stage startups in India and how policy interventions can address these funding gaps.
Evaluate the broader economic impact of a thriving startup ecosystem on job creation, technological advancement, and inclusive growth in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A group of young entrepreneurs collaborating in a modern office space, symbolizing startup innovation.
GS3.trivia: India is home to the third-largest startup ecosystem in the world.

GS3.headline: Amrit Sarovar Mission Exceeds Targets, Focus Shifts to Maintenance and Community Ownership
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Rural Development announced that the 'Amrit Sarovar Mission' has successfully created/revived over 60,000 water bodies across India, surpassing its initial target.
• The mission, launched in 2022, aimed to develop and rejuvenate 75 water bodies in each district as part of Azadi Ka Amrit Mahotsav.
• Focus is now shifting towards ensuring long-term maintenance, community participation, and sustainable utilization of these rejuvenated sarovars.
• These water bodies play a crucial role in groundwater recharge, irrigation, and local biodiversity conservation.
• The success highlights the potential of community-led initiatives in water conservation and rural development.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Water resource management.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
GS3.source: Ministry of Rural Development, DD News
prelimsPoints: Amrit Sarovar Mission was launched in 2022.
Aims to develop/rejuvenate 75 water bodies in each district.
Falls under the Ministry of Rural Development.
mainsPoints: Assess the impact of the 'Amrit Sarovar Mission' on water conservation and rural livelihoods in India. What are the key factors contributing to its success?
Discuss the challenges in ensuring long-term sustainability and community ownership of rejuvenated water bodies, and suggest strategies to overcome them.
Examine how such decentralized water management initiatives contribute to national water security and climate change adaptation strategies.
GS3.imageDescription: Aerial view of a newly rejuvenated 'Amrit Sarovar' with clear water and surrounding greenery.
GS3.trivia: India receives about 4% of the global precipitation, but has 18% of the world's population.

GS3.headline: New Cybersecurity Framework for Critical Infrastructure Sectors Released
GS3.explainer:
• The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) has issued an updated cybersecurity framework for critical infrastructure sectors.
• The framework mandates stricter compliance for sectors like energy, finance, telecommunications, and transport against growing cyber threats.
• It emphasizes proactive threat intelligence sharing, regular security audits, and capacity building for cybersecurity professionals.
• The move is a response to increasing sophisticated cyber-attacks targeting essential services globally.
• Aims to bolster India's cyber resilience and protect national assets from digital sabotage and espionage.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
keyTerms: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsPoints: NCIIPC is responsible for protecting India's Critical Information Infrastructure.
Critical infrastructure sectors include energy, finance, telecom, etc.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for cyber security incidents.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cyber threats to India's critical infrastructure and the necessity of a robust cybersecurity framework.
Analyze the key provisions of the new cybersecurity framework and its potential impact on enhancing India's cyber resilience.
Examine the challenges in implementing comprehensive cybersecurity measures across diverse critical sectors and suggest strategies for effective coordination and capacity building.
GS3.imageDescription: Abstract digital image representing cybersecurity with a shield icon over network lines.
GS3.trivia: The first computer virus, 'Brain', was created in 1986.

GS3.headline: PM GatiShakti National Master Plan Review Highlights Inter-Ministerial Synergy
GS3.explainer:
• A high-level meeting reviewed the progress of the PM GatiShakti National Master Plan, emphasizing improved inter-ministerial coordination.
• The platform integrates infrastructure projects across 16 ministries, aiming for holistic planning and reduced logistics costs.
• Key achievements include faster project approvals, optimized route planning, and identification of critical infrastructure gaps.
• The focus remains on enhancing multimodal connectivity and boosting India's economic competitiveness.
• Challenges related to land acquisition and financing models for large-scale projects were also discussed.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
GS3.source: Ministry of Commerce and Industry, Business Standard
prelimsPoints: PM GatiShakti was launched in 2021.
It is a digital platform integrating 16 ministries for infrastructure planning.
Aims to reduce logistics costs and improve multi-modal connectivity.
mainsPoints: Assess the significance of the PM GatiShakti National Master Plan in transforming India's infrastructure landscape and boosting economic competitiveness.
Discuss how the GatiShakti platform addresses the challenges of inter-ministerial coordination and project delays in large-scale infrastructure development.
Examine the potential impact of improved logistics and multi-modal connectivity on various sectors of the Indian economy, including manufacturing, agriculture, and exports.
GS3.imageDescription: A complex network of roads, railways, ports, and airports overlaid with digital lines, representing PM GatiShakti.
GS3.trivia: India aims to reduce logistics costs from 13-14% of GDP to 8-10% through initiatives like GatiShakti.

GS3.headline: Indian Antarctic Expedition Underway for Critical Climate Change Research
GS3.explainer:
• The latest Indian scientific expedition to Antarctica is fully operational, focusing on critical climate change research.
• Scientists are collecting data on glacial retreat, sea-level rise, ocean acidification, and atmospheric changes.
• The mission utilizes India's research stations, Maitri and Bharati, for year-round scientific investigations.
• Research contributes to global understanding of polar dynamics and their impact on global weather patterns.
• India is a signatory to the Antarctic Treaty System, committed to peaceful scientific research in the region.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Climate Change.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: India has two operational research stations in Antarctica: Maitri and Bharati.
India is a signatory to the Antarctic Treaty System.
Research focuses on climate change, glaciology, and oceanography.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's scientific expeditions to Antarctica in understanding global climate change and its implications for the Indian subcontinent.
Analyze the role of international cooperation and the Antarctic Treaty System in promoting peaceful scientific research and environmental protection in the polar regions.
Evaluate the challenges faced by polar research missions and the technological advancements required to sustain long-term scientific presence in extreme environments.
GS3.imageDescription: Indian research station 'Bharati' in Antarctica amidst icy landscape, with scientific equipment.
GS3.trivia: India launched its first expedition to Antarctica in 1981.

GS3.headline: India Records Significant Solar Energy Capacity Addition in Q4 2025
GS3.explainer:
• A report by a renewable energy consultancy indicated a substantial increase in India's solar energy capacity addition during the last quarter of 2025.
• The growth is attributed to supportive government policies, declining solar panel costs, and increased private investment.
• This surge contributes significantly to India's ambitious renewable energy targets and climate change commitments.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, land acquisition, and ensuring round-the-clock power supply.
• The push for domestic manufacturing under the PLI scheme is further bolstering the solar sector.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: India aims for 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
PLI scheme supports domestic manufacturing of solar PV modules.
Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) is the nodal ministry.
mainsPoints: Assess the progress and challenges faced by India in achieving its ambitious renewable energy targets, particularly in the solar sector.
Discuss the role of government policies, such as the PLI scheme, and private investment in accelerating solar energy capacity addition in India.
Examine the technical and economic hurdles in integrating large-scale intermittent renewable energy into the national grid and ensuring energy security.
GS3.imageDescription: A vast solar power farm stretching to the horizon under a clear sky.
GS3.trivia: The International Solar Alliance (ISA) was jointly launched by India and France at COP21 in Paris.

GS3.headline: Government Bolsters Support for Biotech Startups in Healthcare Sector
GS3.explainer:
• The Department of Biotechnology (DBT) announced new initiatives to further support biotech startups focusing on healthcare innovation.
• Measures include enhanced funding, mentorship programs, and streamlined regulatory approvals for novel diagnostic tools and therapies.
• The aim is to accelerate the development of affordable and accessible healthcare solutions, especially for neglected diseases.
• This push aligns with the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision, reducing reliance on imported medical technologies.
• Collaboration between academia, industry, and government is crucial for fostering a vibrant biotech ecosystem.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Bio-technology.
GS3.source: Department of Biotechnology, The Hindu BusinessLine
prelimsPoints: DBT is the nodal agency for biotechnology research and development in India.
Atmanirbhar Bharat aims for self-reliance in various sectors, including healthcare.
Biotech startups are crucial for innovation in diagnostics and therapeutics.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of biotechnology startups in driving healthcare innovation and achieving self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat) in the medical sector.
Analyze the challenges faced by biotech startups in India, including funding, regulatory hurdles, and market access, and how government support can mitigate these.
Evaluate the potential of indigenous biotech solutions to address healthcare disparities and improve public health outcomes in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Microscopic view of cells with glowing elements, symbolizing biotechnology research.
GS3.trivia: The first successful gene therapy was performed in 1990.

GS3.headline: Urban Local Bodies Pilot Mitigation Strategies for Urban Heat Island Effect
GS3.explainer:
• Several urban local bodies across India have begun piloting innovative strategies to combat the 'Urban Heat Island' (UHI) effect.
• Initiatives include promoting green roofs, cool pavements, urban forestry, and water-sensitive urban design.
• The UHI effect leads to significantly higher temperatures in urban areas compared to surrounding rural areas, impacting public health and energy consumption.
• These pilot projects aim to develop scalable models for climate-resilient urban planning.
• Community awareness and participation are crucial for the long-term success of these mitigation efforts.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Climate Change.
Disaster and disaster management.
GS3.source: The Hindu, Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect refers to higher temperatures in urban areas.
Factors contributing to UHI include dark surfaces, lack of vegetation, and waste heat.
Mitigation strategies include green roofs, cool pavements, and urban forests.
mainsPoints: Discuss the causes and consequences of the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect in Indian cities, particularly in the context of climate change and public health.
Analyze the effectiveness of various mitigation strategies, such as green infrastructure and cool pavements, in reducing urban temperatures and enhancing climate resilience.
Examine the role of urban local bodies and community participation in implementing sustainable urban planning solutions to combat the UHI effect.
GS3.imageDescription: An aerial view of a city showing a stark contrast between concrete buildings and green spaces, illustrating the UHI effect.
GS3.trivia: The UHI effect can make cities several degrees Celsius warmer than their surrounding rural areas.

sources: Ministry of Rural Development, DD News
Archaeological Survey of India, The Wire
